items.title: Woolacombe Beach
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.summary: North Devon surf spot • Beginner to Intermediate
items.content:
A long sandy beach with plenty of room, regular Atlantic swell and a strong surf culture. Good for learners on smaller days and more experienced surfers when the swell has shape.

  • Vibe: Big, open and popular
  • Experience level: Beginner to Intermediate
  • Best conditions: West to north-west swell; light easterly winds; check sandbanks and tides
  • Access: Easy access from Woolacombe village and seafront
  • Parking: Large paid car parks near the beach

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Putsborough Sands
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.summary: North Devon surf spot • Beginner to Intermediate
items.content:
A protected end of the Woolacombe stretch, often chosen when exposed beaches are wind affected. A strong option for longboarders, improvers and relaxed surf sessions.

  • Vibe: Sheltered, scenic and spacious
  • Experience level: Beginner to Intermediate
  • Best conditions: West/north-west swell; often cleaner in southerly winds; mid tide can be useful
  • Access: Access via Putsborough car park and beach path
  • Parking: Paid beach car park

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Croyde Bay
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.summary: North Devon surf spot • Intermediate to Advanced
items.content:
One of North Devon’s best-known surf beaches, with powerful peaks and a lively surf scene. Excellent on the right day, but not the easiest choice for beginners in bigger surf.

  • Vibe: Iconic, punchy and busy
  • Experience level: Intermediate to Advanced
  • Best conditions: West/north-west swell; offshore easterlies; works best with defined banks
  • Access: Beach access from village paths and car parks
  • Parking: Paid car parks around Croyde

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Saunton Sands
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.summary: North Devon surf spot • Beginner to Intermediate
items.content:
A long, gently sloping beach famous for long rides and learner-friendly waves. A reliable choice for surf schools, longboarders and cruisy sessions.

  • Vibe: Longboard-friendly and mellow
  • Experience level: Beginner to Intermediate
  • Best conditions: West/north-west swell; smaller clean days; mid tide often popular
  • Access: Straightforward access from the main beach entrance
  • Parking: Large paid car park by the beach

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Westward Ho! Beach
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.summary: North Devon surf spot • Beginner to Intermediate
items.content:
A wide beach with plenty of room and exposed Atlantic conditions. It can suit learners in small surf and improvers when the banks are working.

  • Vibe: Open, family-friendly and exposed
  • Experience level: Beginner to Intermediate
  • Best conditions: West/north-west swell; light offshore winds; best with manageable swell
  • Access: Easy access from the promenade and pebble ridge areas
  • Parking: Paid seafront and town parking

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.
items.address: Westward Ho! Beach, North Devon, UK

items.title: Lynmouth
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.content:
A famous North Devon point-style setup that can produce serious waves when conditions line up. Best left to experienced surfers who understand rocks, currents and local conditions.

  • Vibe: Raw, powerful and scenic
  • Experience level: Advanced
  • Best conditions: Strong groundswell with suitable tide and wind; experienced surfers only
  • Access: Access from Lynmouth with care around rocks and harbour areas
  • Parking: Village parking available

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Combesgate Beach
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.content:
A smaller beach close to Woolacombe that can offer surf when the sand and tide line up. Space is limited compared with the main beach.

  • Vibe: Compact, pretty and tide-sensitive
  • Experience level: Intermediate
  • Best conditions: Moderate west/north-west swell; avoid unsuitable tides; check local conditions
  • Access: Steps/path access from Woolacombe area
  • Parking: Use Woolacombe parking options

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Barricane Beach
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.content:
A small cove near Woolacombe with rocky surroundings. It is more of a novelty/sheltered check than a main beginner beach.

  • Vibe: Small, tucked-away and characterful
  • Experience level: Intermediate
  • Best conditions: Small to moderate swell; tide-dependent; check rocks
  • Access: Short walk from Woolacombe
  • Parking: Use nearby Woolacombe parking

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.

items.title: Hartland Quay
items.author: CodeSurf Solutions
items.content:
A dramatic, exposed coastline spot where conditions can be heavy and changeable. Only suitable for experienced water users with local knowledge.

  • Vibe: Remote, rugged and powerful
  • Experience level: Advanced
  • Best conditions: Large Atlantic swell windows; only in safe, suitable conditions
  • Access: Steep rugged access; check tide and sea state carefully
  • Parking: Limited parking near Hartland Quay

Check surf forecast | Search live webcam | Parking information

Safety note: surf conditions change quickly. Check tide, wind, swell and local lifeguard guidance before entering the water.
